content.text: Main research areas
Alignment & safety science
How to make models follow human values and avoid harmful behavior, including Constitutional AI and oversight methods.
Interpretability
Understanding what’s going on inside large models (circuits, features, internal goals) so they are more transparent and controllable.
Evaluation & auditing
Designing tests and audits to detect dangerous capabilities or “hidden objectives” in models.
Economics, governance, and societal impact
Studying how frontier AI affects the economy, labor, and policy, and how to govern it responsibly.
Example flagship publications
Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback
Paper describing Anthropic’s “Constitutional AI” method, where a written set of principles guides the model’s behavior and training instead of relying only on human labels.
Collective Constitutional AI
Extends the idea by incorporating public or diverse input into the constitution used to steer the model, increasing legitimacy and robustness.
Auditing language models for hidden objectives
Research on checking whether models have internal goals or behaviors that are different from what we intend, and how to detect them.
Automated alignment researchers
Work on using AI systems themselves to help with alignment research, making safety work scale alongside model capabilities.
Alignment & interpretability talks and salons
Anthropic runs public “Research Salon” events and talks on alignment, interpretability, and future risks, which are a good entry point for learners.
